result.stdout:
USAGE:
    toolkit [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

EXAMPLES:
    toolkit command-exists curl
    toolkit command-exists foo -t -l Fatal
    toolkit selfupdate

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

COMMANDS:
    command-exists <CommandName>     
    setenv-gitroot                   
    setenv-gitbranch                 
    nuget-delist <PackageName>       
    selfupdate

USAGE:

EXAMPLES:
    toolkit command-exists curl
    toolkit command-exists foo -t -l Fatal
    toolkit selfupdate

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

COMMANDS:
    command-exists <CommandName>

    nuget-delist <PackageName>

result.stdout:
USAGE:
    toolkit command-exists <CommandName> [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    toolkit command-exists curl
    toolkit command-exists foo -t -l Fatal

ARGUMENTS:
    <CommandName>    The shell commandline command e.g. cmd,curl,bash

OPTIONS:
                        DEFAULT                                                 
    -h, --help                         Prints help information                  
    -l, --loglevel      Information    Minimum loglevel, valid values =>        
                                       Verbose,Debug,Information,Warning,Error,F
                                       atal                                     
    -t, --throwError                   Throws and errorcode if command is not   
                                       found

result.stdout:
USAGE:
    toolkit setenv-gitbranch [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
                          DEFAULT                                               
    -h, --help                           Prints help information                
    -l, --loglevel        Information    Minimum loglevel, valid values =>      
                                         Verbose,Debug,Information,Warning,Error
                                         ,Fatal                                 
    -t, --throwError                     Throws and errorcode if unsuccessfull  
    -g, --githubaction                   Writes the output as plain text, that  
                                         can be used to set an                  
                                         enviromentvariable "toolkit -g         
                                         setenv-gitbranch >> $GITHUB_ENV "

commands.payload:
